title: Add rusage-logfile flag to optionally send rusage to a file
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind feature


#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Currently the rusage is reported to stdout and rather cumbersome to parse. The
new flag rusage-logfile can be used to specify a file to which the log will be
written instead.

#### How to verify it

Run `buildah bud --log-rusage --rusage-logfile "tests.log"`

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None


#### Special notes for your reviewer:

I'd like to add a bats integration test as well, but am unfamiliar with bats. If anyone could help me out with that, that would be greatly appreciated.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
- add --rusage-logfile flag to log rusage into a file
```

comments.body: Simplest thing for testing would be to add the flag to one of the existing bud tests and make sure it records data to the specified file.  Bats is just a fancy wrapper on bash.

/approve
I am not a big fan of options container "-" but since this is hidden, I am fine with it.

> I am not a big fan of options container "-" but since this is hidden, I am fine with it.

I have no strong opinion on this, so I can change it to something else. I just tried to adhere to the already existing options and didn't come up with a shorter name.
Looks like you need to rebase @dcermak
